data.content:
### Email Template for User Invite in Supabase Auth Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md HTML template for user invitation emails. Uses template variables like SiteURL, Email, and ConfirmationURL. Displays a link to accept the invite. Falls back to this default if custom template is unavailable. ```html

You have been invited

You have been invited to create a user on {{ .SiteURL }}. Follow this link to accept the invite:

Accept the invite

``` -------------------------------- ### POST /invite Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md Invite a new user with an email. Requires service_role or supabase_admin JWT. ```APIDOC ## POST /invite ### Description Invites a new user with an email. This endpoint requires the `service_role` or `supabase_admin` JWT. ### Method POST ### Endpoint /invite ### Parameters #### Headers - **Authorization** (string) - Required - Bearer token with service_role or supabase_admin JWT #### Request Body - **email** (string) - Required - User's email address ### Request Example ```json { "email": "email@example.com" } ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **id** (string) - User ID - **email** (string) - User's email - **confirmation_sent_at** (string) - Timestamp when confirmation was sent - **created_at** (string) - Timestamp when user was created - **updated_at** (string) - Timestamp when user was last updated - **invited_at** (string) - Timestamp when user was invited #### Response Example ```json { "id": "11111111-2222-3333-4444-5555555555555", "email": "email@example.com", "confirmation_sent_at": "2016-05-15T20:49:40.882805774-07:00", "created_at": "2016-05-15T19:53:12.368652374-07:00", "updated_at": "2016-05-15T19:53:12.368652374-07:00", "invited_at": "2016-05-15T19:53:12.368652374-07:00" } ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure SMTP Settings for Supabase Auth Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md Sets up SMTP server configuration for sending emails in Supabase Auth. Requires host, port, user, password, admin email, and subject. Inputs are environment variables for mail server details. Outputs enable email functionality for auth features like confirmations. ```properties GOTRUE_SMTP_HOST=smtp.mandrillapp.com GOTRUE_SMTP_PORT=587 GOTRUE_SMTP_USER=smtp-delivery@example.com GOTRUE_SMTP_PASS=correcthorsebatterystaple GOTRUE_SMTP_ADMIN_EMAIL=support@example.com GOTRUE_MAILER_SUBJECTS_CONFIRMATION="Please confirm" ``` -------------------------------- ### POST /admin/generate_link Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md This endpoint generates an email action link based on the specified type, such as signup, magiclink, recovery, or invite. It requires admin authorization and includes details like the action link, email OTP, hashed token, verification type, and redirect URL. The response provides query parameters separately for convenience along with the email OTP and hashed token. ```APIDOC ## POST /admin/generate_link ### Description Generates an email action link based on the type (signup, magiclink, recovery, or invite) and returns the link along with query parameters, email OTP, hashed token, and verification details. Requires admin authorization. ### Method POST ### Endpoint /admin/generate_link ### Parameters #### Path Parameters None #### Query Parameters None #### Request Body - **type** (string) - Required - The type of link to generate: 'signup', 'magiclink', 'recovery', or 'invite'. - **email** (string) - Required - The email address for the link. - **password** (string) - Optional - User's password, required if type is 'signup'. - **data** (object) - Optional - Additional signup data, required if type is 'signup'. - **redirect_to** (string) - Optional - Redirect URL after email action, defaults to SITE_URL. ### Request Example { "type": "signup", "email": "email@example.com", "password": "secret", "data": { ... }, "redirect_to": "https://supabase.io" } ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **action_link** (string) - The full action link URL. - **email_otp** (string) - The email OTP code. - **hashed_token** (string) - The hashed token. - **verification_type** (string) - The type of verification. - **redirect_to** (string) - The redirect URL after action. #### Response Example { "action_link": "http://localhost:9999/verify?token=TOKEN&type=TYPE&redirect_to=REDIRECT_URL", "email_otp": "EMAIL_OTP", "hashed_token": "TOKEN", "verification_type": "TYPE", "redirect_to": "REDIRECT_URL", ... } ``` -------------------------------- ### POST /admin/generate_link Endpoint in JavaScript Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md Generates email action links for types like signup, magiclink, recovery, or invite. Requires admin JWT authorization. Body specifies type, email, optional password/data, and redirect_to URL; defaults to SITE_URL. Returns action_link, email_otp, hashed_token, and verification details. ```javascript headers: { "Authorization": "Bearer eyJhbGciOiJI...M3A90LCkxxtX9oNP9KZO" // admin role required } body: { "type": "signup" or "magiclink" or "recovery" or "invite", "email": "email@example.com", "password": "secret", // only if type = signup "data": { ... }, // only if type = signup "redirect_to": "https://supabase.io" // Redirect URL to send the user to after an email action. Defaults to SITE_URL. } Returns { "action_link": "http://localhost:9999/verify?token=TOKEN&type=TYPE&redirect_to=REDIRECT_URL", "email_otp": "EMAIL_OTP", "hashed_token": "TOKEN", "verification_type": "TYPE", "redirect_to": "REDIRECT_URL", ... } ``` -------------------------------- ### Email Change Confirmation Template Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md HTML template for confirming email address changes with variables for SiteURL, Email, NewEmail, and ConfirmationURL. Used when users request to update their email address. ```html

Confirm Change of Email

Follow this link to confirm the update of your email from {{ .Email }} to {{ .NewEmail }}:

Change Email

``` -------------------------------- ### Email Address Changed Notification Template Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md HTML template for notifying users when their email address has been updated, with Email and OldEmail variables for tracking the change. ```html

Your email address has been changed

The email address for your account has been changed from {{ .OldEmail }} to {{ .Email }}.

If you did not make this change, please contact support.

``` -------------------------------- ### Email Template for Signup Confirmation in Supabase Auth Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md HTML template for signup confirmation emails. Uses template variables like SiteURL, Email, and ConfirmationURL. Displays a link to confirm the user account. Falls back to this default if custom template is unavailable. ```html

Confirm your signup

Follow this link to confirm your user:

Confirm your mail

``` -------------------------------- ### Send Magic Link - JSON Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md This endpoint delivers a magic link to the user's email address, allowing them to redeem an access token. It's recommended to use /otp instead for improved security. The input is a JSON object containing the user's email address. The endpoint returns an empty response. ```json { "email": "email@example.com" } ``` -------------------------------- ### Email Template for Magic Link Login in Supabase Auth Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md HTML template for magic link emails. Uses template variables like SiteURL, Email, and ConfirmationURL. Displays a link to log in via magic link. Falls back to this default if custom template is unavailable. ```html

Magic Link

Follow this link to login:

Log In

``` -------------------------------- ### Email Template for Password Recovery in Supabase Auth Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md HTML template for password reset emails. Uses template variables like SiteURL, Email, and ConfirmationURL. Displays a link to reset the password. Falls back to this default if custom template is unavailable. ```html

Reset Password

Follow this link to reset the password for your user:

Reset Password

``` -------------------------------- ### POST /magiclink Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md Sends a magic link to the user's email address, which can be used to redeem an access token. Note: It's recommended to use `/otp` instead. Rate limited to once every 60 seconds. ```APIDOC ## POST /magiclink ### Description Sends a magic link to the user's email address for authentication. ### Method POST ### Endpoint /magiclink ### Parameters #### Request Body - **email** (string) - Required - The email address of the user. ### Request Example ```json { "email": "email@example.com" } ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) Returns an empty JSON object upon successful request. #### Response Example ```json {} ``` **Note**: The magic link, when clicked, redirects to `#access_token=x&refresh_token=y&expires_in=z&token_type=bearer&type=magiclink`. ``` -------------------------------- ### PUT /user Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md Updates a user's profile information, including email, password, phone, and custom data. Changing email triggers a magic link email. Requires authentication. ```APIDOC ## PUT /user ### Description Updates the profile of the currently authenticated user. This can include changing email, password, phone number, or setting custom user data. ### Method PUT ### Endpoint /user ### Parameters Requires authentication via the `Authorization` header. #### Request Body - **email** (string) - Optional - The new email address for the user. Changing this will send a verification email. - **password** (string) - Optional - The new password for the user. - **phone** (string) - Optional - The new phone number for the user. - **data** (object) - Optional - A JSON object to store custom user data. - **key** (any) - Example custom data field. - **number** (integer) - Example custom data field. - **admin** (boolean) - Example custom data field. ### Request Example ```json { "email": "new-email@example.com", "password": "new-password", "phone": "+123456789", "data": { "key": "value", "number": 10, "admin": false } } ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **id** (string) - The user's ID. - **email** (string) - The user's email. - **email_change_sent_at** (string) - Timestamp when the email change verification was sent. - **phone** (string) - The user's phone number. - **phone_change_sent_at** (string) - Timestamp when the phone change verification was sent. - **created_at** (string) - Timestamp when the user account was created. - **updated_at** (string) - Timestamp when the user account was last updated. #### Response Example ```json { "id": "11111111-2222-3333-4444-5555555555555", "email": "email@example.com", "email_change_sent_at": "2016-05-15T20:49:40.882805774-07:00", "phone": "+123456789", "phone_change_sent_at": "2016-05-15T20:49:40.882805774-07:00", "created_at": "2016-05-15T19:53:12.368652374-07:00", "updated_at": "2016-05-15T19:53:12.368652374-07:00" } ``` **Security Note**: If `GOTRUE_SECURITY_UPDATE_PASSWORD_REQUIRE_REAUTHENTICATION` is enabled, reauthentication with a nonce will be required before updating the password. The request body would then include `password` and `nonce`. ``` -------------------------------- ### Reauthenticate - Headers Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md This endpoint sends a nonce to the user's email or phone, requiring the user to be logged in/authenticated. Requires an Authorization header with a bearer token. This endpoint requires the user to have either an email or phone number. ```javascript headers: { "Authorization" : "Bearer eyJhbGciOiJI...M3A90LCkxxtX9oNP9KZO" } ```
